Released: 8 May 2026

This release of EDB Postgres® AI for CloudNativePG™ Cluster includes the following:

## Highlights

**`CVE-2026-44477` / `GHSA-423p-g724-fr39`: metrics exporter privilege
escalation.** The metrics exporter no longer authenticates as the
`postgres` superuser. It now uses a dedicated `cnp_metrics_exporter` role
with `pg_monitor` privileges only, closing a chain that let a low-privilege
database user gain PostgreSQL superuser.
([`GHSA-423p-g724-fr39`](https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/security/advisories/GHSA-423p-g724-fr39))

Upgrade impact: custom monitoring queries that read user-owned tables, or
use `target_databases: '*'` against databases where `PUBLIC CONNECT` has
been revoked, need explicit `GRANT` statements to `cnp_metrics_exporter`.
See ["Custom query privileges and safety"](../monitoring/#custom-query-privileges-and-safety)
and ["Manually creating the metrics exporter role"](../monitoring/#manually-creating-the-metrics-exporter-role)
in the monitoring documentation.

For replica clusters, upgrade the source primary cluster before any
replica clusters that consume from it. The `cnp_metrics_exporter` role
is created on the source primary and replicates downstream; a replica
cluster upgraded first will scrape against a missing role until the
source primary upgrades. The manual-recovery section linked above also
covers replica clusters.

## Security Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Schema-qualified catalog references in default monitoring queries
</summary><hr/><p>Hardened the shipped monitoring configuration and documentation samples by
qualifying every <code>pg_catalog</code> object explicitly. Unqualified references
resolve through <code>search_path</code>, which a database user can manipulate to
shadow built-in objects.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10576">#10576</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Discoverable SBOM and provenance attestations
</summary><hr/><p>SBOM and SLSA provenance attached to operator container images now follow
the OCI 1.1 Referrers spec, so standard registry tooling and supply-chain
scanners can discover them automatically.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10601">#10601</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>CVE remediation in <code>github.com/jackc/pgx/v5</code>
</summary><hr/><p>Bumped to v5.9.2 to pick up upstream fixes for <code>CVE-2026-33816</code>
(memory-safety in <code>pgproto3</code>) and <code>GHSA-j88v-2chj-qfwx</code> (SQL injection via
simple-protocol dollar-quoted string handling).</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10436">#10436</a>, <a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10498">#10498</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>CVE remediation in the Go runtime
</summary><hr/><p>Built with Go 1.26.3 to pick up upstream fixes in <code>crypto/x509</code>,
<code>crypto/tls</code>, <code>net/http</code>, and <code>net</code> (CVE-2026-32280, CVE-2026-32281,
CVE-2026-33810, CVE-2026-33814, CVE-2026-33811, CVE-2026-39825).</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10462">#10462</a>, <a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10647">#10647</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Build pipeline hardening
</summary><hr/><p>The Go 1.26.3 bump also addresses CVE-2026-42501 (<code>cmd/go</code> module-checksum
validation), reducing supply-chain exposure during release builds. The
affected code paths are not reachable from the running operator.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10647">#10647</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Changes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Switched TLS peer verification from <code>VerifyPeerCertificate</code> to
<code>VerifyConnection</code>, which runs on every completed handshake (the former is
skipped on resumed TLS 1.3 sessions).
</summary><hr/><p>Session resumption is not enabled in EDB Postgres for Kubernetes today, so
this has no observable effect, but it future-proofs verification if
session caching is introduced later.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10478">#10478</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Bug Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a failover window where the former primary kept its primary label.
</summary><hr/><p>If the former primary returned during failover (for example, after a
transient network partition), the <code>-rw</code> service kept routing to it,
replicas could reconnect, and committed writes were lost to <code>pg_rewind</code>.
The old primary is now labeled <code>unhealthy</code> to isolate it from service
traffic during failover.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10409">#10409</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed failover not being triggered when the node hosting the primary
becomes unreachable.
</summary><hr/><p>The operator now reads the pod's <code>Ready</code> condition (flipped to <code>False</code> by
the node controller when the kubelet stops reporting) instead of
<code>ContainersReady</code>, which stays stale as <code>True</code> in that scenario. Combined
with the spurious-failover guard
(<a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10445">#10445</a>),
failover triggers only when Kubernetes itself marks the pod not Ready.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10448">#10448</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed spurious failovers caused by transient failures on the primary's
HTTP status endpoint.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10445">#10445</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed escaping of backslashes and control characters in PostgreSQL
configuration values.
</summary><hr/><p>Previously, such characters in parameters like <code>log_line_prefix</code> could
corrupt the configuration file or be silently stripped at runtime.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10515">#10515</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>restore_command</code> construction to shell-quote each argument.
</summary><hr/><p>Values such as a <code>destinationPath</code> containing whitespace (for example,
<code>s3://my bucket/wal</code>) were word-split by the POSIX shell and passed to the
WAL restore tool as separate arguments.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10518">#10518</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Tightened <code>recoveryTarget</code> validation in the admission webhook.
</summary><hr/><p><code>targetXID</code> must now be a non-negative 32-bit integer, and <code>targetName</code>
must be shorter than 64 bytes and free of ASCII control characters.
Malformed values are rejected at admission instead of failing later during
PostgreSQL recovery.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10565">#10565</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed snapshot restores failing when leftover `pgsql_tmp*` directories
were present in the data directory.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10447">#10447</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed a deadlock occurring when PVC storage size and resource requests
are changed simultaneously.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10427">#10427</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
